probe_surface: EEOC Public Portal explainer page on eeoc.gov
observable_public_signal: Public EEOC explainer page is reachable and links to the official portal surface.
why_it_matters: Shows that a genuinely different official EEOC route exists beyond the newsroom pages and EDNY iQuery route, so later blocked access can be described as a tested official-surface barrier rather than an untried navigation path.
what_it_does_not_prove: Does not provide case-specific court text, decree text, or searchable portal results for the iTutorGroup matter.
paper_thesis_use: Methods/limitations note distinguishing official-route existence from anonymous usability.
next_safe_route: Use only as a route-governance companion to the open-court probes; do not treat the explainer page as evidence depth.

probe_surface: Official EEOC Public Portal target reached through browser interaction
observable_public_signal: Browser session reached an Imperva block page exposing proxy/IP metadata and an incident ID before any case-search or filing surface became usable.
why_it_matters: Strengthens the official-access-barrier note for `sf08`: one different EEOC-hosted route was tested live and still prevented anonymous portal use on this host before any case-level lookup or document retrieval could begin.
what_it_does_not_prove: Does not prove that the portal contains or does not contain iTutorGroup case materials; it only proves host-side/browser-side anonymous access failure on this route.
paper_thesis_use: Reviewer-safe negative evidence for hostile-reviewer questions about whether another official EEOC route was tried.
next_safe_route: Do not repeat this same anonymous browser route unless host/network conditions change; prioritize only a genuinely different official open surface or clearly labeled public mirror.

probe_surface: Direct anonymous GET to the official EEOC Public Portal target
observable_public_signal: Anonymous shell retrieval returned HTTP 403 with Incapsula/Imperva HTML instead of a searchable public portal page.
why_it_matters: Confirms that the barrier is not limited to one browser rendering quirk: both browser and shell paths hit access control before any case-specific artifact was exposed.
what_it_does_not_prove: Does not convert the portal into a court-originating source and does not establish absence of open court documents elsewhere.
paper_thesis_use: Methods appendix row separating host access friction from source absence.
next_safe_route: Carry this row with the existing EEOC open-court probe artifacts whenever manuscript/dashboard/thesis prose mentions official-route barriers for `LIMEN-000002`.
